title: New York Is About to Feel Hotter Than Phoenix
gist: An extreme humid heat wave pushing NYC heat-index toward 109F is being intensified by fossil-fuel-driven warming and a newly declared, unusually potent El Niño, with dangerous overnight lows and a summer of more to come.

insights.claim: Burning fossil fuels has made nearly every heat wave more intense than it would have been in a preindustrial climate, pushing moderate heat into extreme heat.
insights.speaker: Isabella Ward
insights.so_what: Reinforces the structural, recurring nature of extreme-heat risk relevant to energy demand and insurance.
insights.quote: The warming from rising greenhouse gases is clearly adding to global temperature, and that adds extra heat to the heat waves
insights.locator: The blast of extreme heat comes a week after Europe

insights.claim: A newly declared and expected-to-be-potent El Niño is boosting temperatures across the northern US and Canada and is forecast to strengthen through summer 2026.
insights.speaker: Isabella Ward
insights.so_what: A strengthening El Niño signals elevated cooling-demand and heat-event probability for the rest of summer.
insights.quote: it's expected to be a particularly potent iteration that will only strengthen as summer goes on

insights.claim: NYC temperatures near 100F could feel like 109F with humidity, which hampers sweating—the body's most powerful cooling mechanism—making humid heat waves physiologically far more dangerous than dry heat like Phoenix's.
insights.speaker: Isabella Ward
insights.so_what: Humid heat carries outsized mortality/health risk; a driver of emergency-response and healthcare demand.
insights.quote: When there's high humidity, especially in a heat wave, it's much more difficult for the body to physiologically cool off

insights.claim: The National Weather Service warns that consecutive hot days with little overnight relief compound heat stress, making high overnight lows particularly problematic during this event.
insights.speaker: Isabella Ward
insights.so_what: Sustained overnight heat raises 24-hour cooling load and vulnerable-population mortality risk.
insights.quote: Several days in a row of hot temperatures with little relief from overnight low temperatures can increase heat stress on the human body
insights.locator: The National Weather Service map of warnings

insights.claim: NYC Mayor Zohran Mamdani urged residents to make a heat plan centered on securing air-conditioning access and checking on vulnerable neighbors.
insights.speaker: Isabella Ward
insights.so_what: Municipal messaging underscores AC access as the frontline adaptation—relevant to grid load and cooling infrastructure.
insights.locator: That danger was underscored by New York mayor Zohran Mamdani
